mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150,000 new jobs signals strong labor market demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative when PAYEMS exceeds 150,000 for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) above 1.1 million units indicate improving consumer confidence and demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative despite HOUST exceeding 1,100,000 units for 2 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below 104.5 signals manufacturing weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive when INDPRO remains below 104.5 for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in CPI (CPIAUCSL) and FEDFUNDS signal disinflationary pressures, creating a favorable environment for equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are negative when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS decline simultaneously for 2 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in real sales (RSAFS) above $470 billion combined with rising GDP (GDPC1) indicates robust economic growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are negative despite RSAFS > 470B and GDPC1 growth exceeding 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are positive when UNRATE > 4.5% and PAYEMS declines for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 105.0 during a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 2%) signals stagflation risks, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive when INDPRO < 105.0 and FEDFUNDS > 2% for 2 consecutive months.
